summary: DHH reports on Dave Thomas's presentation about Ruby at Amazon, where the audience was predominantly C/C++ and Java developers. Despite initial skepticism from engineers accustomed to handling massive scale (32 million customers), Thomas won over the crowd by using Amazon's own domain for his Ruby examples. The presentation included a section on Active Record, the ORM component of Rails. DHH uses the post to invite interested Amazon engineers to reach out about Rails.
key_points: Dave Thomas presented Ruby to a skeptical audience of C/C++ and Java developers at Amazon
Using domain-specific examples relevant to the audience (Amazon's business) proved effective in overcoming skepticism toward new technology
Active Record and Rails were highlighted as key parts of the presentation
DHH actively sought to recruit interest from Amazon engineers toward Rails adoption
money_quotes: When you're used to dealing with 32 million customers, I guess you do develop somewhat of a thick skin towards claims from new technology.
Once Dave drew in the examples with the Amazon domain, the crowd was somewhat more easily swayed.
key_insight: Demonstrating new technology using an audience's own domain and problems is the most effective way to overcome entrenched skepticism from experienced engineers.
